title: Sindhu Answers Doubters with Japan Open Crown, Expresses Tears of Joy
indian_angle: PV Sindhu's victory at the Japan Open marks a significant return to form for the Indian badminton star, silencing critics and boosting national morale.
summary: PV Sindhu clinched the Japan Open title, emotionally reflecting on her journey back to the top after facing doubts about her career.

title: How Cryptic Virat Kohli Remark Sparked PV Sindhu's Revival
indian_angle: This article explores the unexpected influence of Indian cricket captain Virat Kohli on PV Sindhu's recent resurgence and Japan Open triumph.
summary: A cryptic remark from cricket star Virat Kohli reportedly played a role in PV Sindhu's mental and on-court revival, leading to her Japan Open victory.

title: BAI Releases Eligibility Criteria for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has issued crucial guidelines for players aspiring to represent India at the upcoming World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: The BAI has published the eligibility criteria for Indian players aiming for selection in the World Junior Championships 2026 squad for the event in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships Needs Gen Z, Gen Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: The article discusses the need to attract younger generations of fans to the upcoming BWF World Championships 2026, which will be hosted in Delhi, India.
summary: An opinion piece highlights the importance of engaging Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences to ensure the success and future popularity of the BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Implemented in Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has introduced a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, impacting young Indian shuttlers.
summary: The BAI has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships across U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups.

title: BAI Announces Grassroot Level Coaches Development Program
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India is running a Coaches Development Program at the grassroot level in Guwahati and Raipur, fostering future Indian talent.
summary: The BAI has circulated a program for grassroot level coaches development, with sessions scheduled in NCE Guwahati and Raipur from July 15th to 24th, 2026.
